title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Mrs. Laura Eastbrook and Colonel Archibald Eastbrooke discuss the disappearance of the Colonel's German souvenir revolver. Mrs. Eastbrook recalls seeing the weapon in a drawer on Saturday the 30th, the day after the robbery at Miss Blacklock's house. The Colonel expresses relief that the gun was still present after the burglary, as he had previously feared it was stolen by a Swiss man with mob connections. He admits he does not possess a gun license and kept the revolver merely as a war memento. The couple speculates that Mrs. Bart might have taken the weapon for protection following the robbery, but they decide against mentioning it to anyone.

Elsewhere, Miss Marple walks through the village using the vicar's walking stick. After observing the window display at Mr. Elliott's antique shop, she enters the "Bluebird" coffee house and meets Miss Dora Bunner. They discuss local residents, including a widow and tenant named Philly Hems and Edmund Swithinham. Miss Marple inquires about the deep friendship between Miss Bunner and Miss Letitia Blacklock. 

The conversation shifts to the hardships of poverty, and Miss Bunner becomes emotional while describing the struggles of hunger, inadequate clothing, and the difficulty of finding work and paying rent. Miss Bunner reveals that she wrote to Miss Blacklock after seeing her name in the newspaper regarding a luncheon for Milchester Hospital. She notes that Miss Blacklock was a clever girl who once served as a secretary to a wealthy man named Mr. Goddard, and she reached out to her as an old school friend.
